essay: Module 4 Part B — Anxiety in Children and Adolescents: A Systemic Disorder

entries.concept: UK prevalence of child/adolescent anxiety (~1 in 12); EBSA rise post-pandemic

usages.quote: roughly one in twelve in the United Kingdom at any given time (NPMH M4 W4 P51)
School-based anxiety, including emotionally-based school avoidance, also appears to have risen markedly in the post-pandemic period (NPMH M4 W4 P51)

entries.source: Anxiety Disorders in Children and Adolescents   Kowalchuk et al

entries.concept: CBT adaptations by developmental stage; adolescent autonomy constraining parental involvement

usages.quote: family involvement is consistently associated with improved outcomes… endorsed in evidence-based guidelines (Wang et al., 2017; NPMH M4 W4 P56)
adolescents' developmental need for autonomy is likely to constrain parental involvement at older ages (Lebowitz et al., 2019; NPMH M4 W4 P56)

entries.source:
Adapting cognitive behaviour therapy for children and adolescents  Source: Stallard
Early Treatment Response in Children and Adolescents Receiving CBT for Trauma  Source: Wamser-Nanney et al.

entries.concept: vmPFC maturation into early adulthood; neurobiological basis of adolescent vulnerability to fear-learning

usages.quote: The ventromedial prefrontal cortex… continues to mature into early adulthood (Casey et al., 2015; NPMH M4 W1 P06)

entries.notes:
The secret life of the teenage brain :  Blakemore Youtube!
Your brain from birth to death:   The Economist YOUTUBE!  
better find other more credible source!

entries.concept: Stepped-care framework / continuum of need (universal → targeted → specialist)

usages.quote: A stepped-care framework, grounded in the continuum of need, offers the organising logic for a systemic response (NPMH M4 W3 P35)

entries.notes:
The checkup uk: Understanding the Continuum of Need: A Guide for UK Citizens
